question: How do I register for courses and what is the deadline?
answer: Course registration is completed online through the Student Portal. Go to Academic → Course Registration, select courses according to your program structure, and click Submit. The deadline for regular registration is the 2nd Friday of the semester. Late registration incurs a $50 fee and is open until the 3rd Friday.
aliases: when is the deadline to register for classes
how do i sign up for courses
last day to register for modules
how to enroll in classes this semester

question: Can I add or drop courses after the semester starts?
answer: Yes. You may add or drop courses during the first two weeks of the semester (Add/Drop period) via the Student Portal. No schedule changes are permitted after the end of the second week.
aliases: can i change my courses after lectures start

question: What happens if I register late for courses?
answer: Late registration is allowed from the Monday after the regular deadline until the 3rd Friday of the semester. A late registration fee of $50 applies. Courses registered late may have limited seat availability.

question: What is the grading system and what happens if I fail a course?
answer: The university uses letter grades: A (70–100%, GPA 5.0), B (60–69%, 4.0), C (50–59%, 3.0), D (40–49%, 2.0), F (0–39%, 0.0). If you fail (grade F), you must re-register and retake the course the next time it is offered. A course may be retaken at most three times.
aliases: what grade do i need to pass
can i retake a failed module

question: What happens if I miss an exam due to illness?
answer: If you miss an exam due to a medical emergency, submit a medical report signed by the University Medical Officer to the Registry within 48 hours of the exam. If approved, you may sit a Special Examination during the break period.
aliases: missed exam because i was sick
rules for exam cancellations or sickness

question: What are the rules on cheating and academic misconduct during exams?
answer: Cheating, plagiarism, and bringing unauthorized materials into an exam are serious offences. Penalties range from zero marks for the paper to suspension or expulsion, depending on severity. Report suspected misconduct to the Examinations Office.

question: When do end-of-semester examinations take place?
answer: Revision Week runs December 1–7. End-of-semester examinations are held December 8–22. Individual timetables are published on the Student Portal two weeks before exams begin.

question: How many books can I borrow from the library and for how long?
answer: Undergraduates may borrow up to 4 books for 14 days. Postgraduates may borrow up to 8 books for 30 days. Renewals can be made online twice unless another user has reserved the book. Overdue fines are $1 per day per book.
aliases: how long can i keep library books

question: What are the library opening hours?
answer: The main library is open Monday–Friday 8:00 AM–8:00 PM and Saturday 9:00 AM–4:00 PM. It is closed on Sundays and public holidays. Extended hours during exam week are announced on the library notice board and portal.

question: How do I connect to campus Wi-Fi and reset my portal password?
answer: Connect to the Uni-Wifi network using your student email (e.g. registrationNumber@student.university.edu) and portal password. To reset your password, open the Student Portal login page, click Forgot Password, and follow the email link. For other ICT issues visit the Helpdesk at Admin Block Room 102 or email support@university.edu.

question: How do I access the Student Portal and e-learning platform?
answer: Log in at portal.university.edu with your student ID and password. The e-learning (LMS) link appears on the portal dashboard after login. First-time users must activate their account using the credentials sent during admission.

question: How do I apply for a hostel and what are the hostel fees?
answer: Hostel applications open 30 days before each semester on the Student Portal. Allocation is first-come, first-served with priority for first-year and international students. The hostel fee is $350 per semester, payable in full before room allocation is confirmed.
aliases: how to get a room on campus

question: What items am I allowed to bring to the hostel?
answer: Students may bring personal bedding, study lamp, and approved electrical appliances (rated 1000W or less). Cooking stoves, pets, and illegal substances are prohibited. A full checklist is on the Accommodation Office portal page.

question: What are the fee payment options and is there an instalment plan?
answer: Tuition may be paid by bank deposit, bank transfer, or online via the Student Portal. Instalment plan: 60% must be paid to register for courses at semester start; the remaining 40% before final examinations. Unpaid fees block access to the exam card.
aliases: can i pay fees in installments

question: Where can I get a receipt or proof of fee payment?
answer: After online payment, download the receipt from Student Portal → Finance → Payment History. For bank deposits, present the teller slip at the Bursary within 48 hours to have your account credited and a receipt issued.

question: What are the key dates for the current academic calendar?
answer: Current semester: Lectures begin September 1; Mid-Semester Break October 15–22; Lectures end November 30; Revision Week December 1–7; Examinations December 8–22. Full calendar PDF is on the university website under Academics.

question: What are the rules regarding dress code and student ID cards?
answer: Students must dress decently and professionally on campus. Inappropriate clothing (e.g. extremely short skirts, ripped jeans, sleeveless tops in lecture halls) is not permitted. Student ID cards must be worn and visible at all times on university premises. Lost IDs are replaced at Security Office for $15.

question: How do I report harassment or a conduct violation?
answer: Report harassment or misconduct to the Dean of Students Office (Student Centre, Room 201) or email conduct@university.edu. Reports are confidential. Emergency situations on campus should also be reported to Security at extension 911.

question: What can you help me with?
answer: I can help with course registration, examination rules, library services, ICT and portal support, hostel applications, fee payment, the academic calendar, and student conduct. Ask me anything about these university services.
